controls.title: AI policy
controls.description: A formal AI policy shall be established, documented, and communicated. The policy includes commitment to responsible AI development/use, alignment with organisational values and legal obligations, and a framework for setting AI objectives. Signed by top management.

controls.title: Alignment with other organisational policies
controls.description: AI-specific requirements shall be incorporated into existing organisational policies (HR, procurement, IT, data governance, information security) where those policies affect or are affected by AI systems.

controls.title: Review of the AI policy
controls.description: The AI policy shall be reviewed at planned intervals and when significant changes occur, to ensure its continuing suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness.

controls.title: AI roles and responsibilities
controls.description: Roles and responsibilities for AI governance shall be defined, assigned, communicated, and documented. Includes accountability for individual AI systems, data governance, and AIMS oversight. RACI matrix recommended.

controls.title: Reporting of concerns
controls.description: Mechanisms shall be established for personnel to raise concerns about AI systems — ethical concerns, bias observations, unexpected outputs, policy non-compliance — without fear of reprisal.

controls.title: Resource documentation
controls.description: Resources required to develop, deploy, operate, and maintain AI systems shall be identified and documented — compute, data, tooling, human resources.

controls.title: Data resources
controls.description: Policies and controls shall govern acquisition, management, and quality assurance of data resources used in AI systems — training, validation, and test data.

controls.title: Tooling resources
controls.description: Controls shall govern selection, procurement, and management of AI tooling — development frameworks, model libraries, evaluation platforms.

controls.title: System and computing resources
controls.description: Controls shall govern compute infrastructure used for AI development and deployment: provisioning, access control, capacity management, decommissioning.

controls.title: Human resources
controls.description: AI-specific competency requirements shall be documented. Training programmes for AI developers, operators, and business users. Awareness programme for all staff interacting with or affected by AI.

controls.title: AI system impact assessment process
controls.description: A documented process for assessing impacts of AI systems shall be established and maintained — defining scope, methodology, frequency, roles, and how assessment results inform control selection and treatment decisions.

controls.title: Documentation of AI system impact assessments
controls.description: Results of AI system impact assessments shall be documented, retained, and updated when significant changes occur to the AI system or its operating context.

controls.title: Impact on individuals or groups
controls.description: Impact assessment shall evaluate how AI outputs may affect individual rights, wellbeing, and autonomy — discrimination, profiling, automated decision-making without meaningful human review, disproportionate impacts on vulnerable groups.

controls.title: Societal impacts
controls.description: Impact assessment shall evaluate broader societal impacts: misinformation generation, labour market effects, environmental impact of compute, reinforcement of systemic bias at scale, erosion of human autonomy.

controls.domain: AI System Life Cycle - Management Guidance
controls.title: Objectives for responsible AI development
controls.description: Specific, measurable objectives for responsible AI development shall be established, documented, and monitored. Align with AI policy and responsible AI principles (fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, reliability).

controls.domain: AI System Life Cycle - Management Guidance
controls.title: Processes for responsible AI design and development
controls.description: Formal processes shall govern AI system design and development to embed responsible AI principles: fairness-aware design, design decisions documentation, explainability proportionate to AI impact level, human oversight at key gates.

controls.domain: AI System Life Cycle - Life Cycle
controls.title: AI system requirements and specification
controls.description: Requirements for each AI system shall be documented — intended purpose, performance criteria, operating conditions, input/output specifications, applicable constraints (legal, ethical, technical).

controls.domain: AI System Life Cycle - Life Cycle
controls.title: Documentation of AI system design and development
controls.description: Design decisions, architecture choices, model versions, hyperparameters, and development rationale shall be documented to support reproducibility, auditability, and accountability.

controls.domain: AI System Life Cycle - Life Cycle
controls.title: AI system verification and validation
controls.description: Testing protocols shall be applied before deployment: performance benchmarking, fairness and bias testing across demographic groups, adversarial testing, edge case validation, go/no-go authorisation criteria.

controls.domain: AI System Life Cycle - Life Cycle
controls.title: AI system deployment
controls.description: Controlled deployment process shall govern AI system releases: staged rollout, deployment authorisation, risk and impact assessment sign-off, rollback procedures.

controls.domain: AI System Life Cycle - Life Cycle
controls.title: AI system operation and monitoring
controls.description: Continuous monitoring of AI systems in production shall detect performance drift, output quality degradation, and bias. Alert thresholds and remediation processes defined.

controls.domain: AI System Life Cycle - Life Cycle
controls.title: AI system technical documentation
controls.description: Comprehensive technical documentation per AI system — specifications, intended use, known limitations, performance data, failure modes. Supports transparency obligations and third-party assessment.

controls.domain: AI System Life Cycle - Life Cycle
controls.title: AI system event logs
controls.description: AI systems shall record event logs sufficient to support incident investigation, audit, performance monitoring, and accountability. Log retention periods and access controls defined.

controls.title: Data for development and enhancement
controls.description: Governance framework shall define how data is managed across the full AI data lifecycle: acquisition, quality assurance, labelling, versioning, bias testing, retention, secure deletion.

controls.title: Acquisition of data
controls.description: Controls shall govern sourcing of training, validation, and test data: legal basis, consent requirements, provenance documentation, prohibition on use of unlawfully obtained data.

controls.title: Quality of data for AI systems
controls.description: Data quality criteria shall be defined for training, validation, and test data — completeness, accuracy, representativeness, recency — and tested before data is used.

controls.title: Data provenance
controls.description: Origin, chain of custody, and transformation history of data used in AI systems shall be documented and traceable. Supports accountability, bias identification, regulatory compliance.

controls.title: Data preparation
controls.description: Processes for data cleaning, transformation, labelling, annotation shall include quality controls: human review of labelled data, inter-annotator agreement checks, identification and remediation of bias introduced in preparation steps.

controls.title: System documentation and information for users
controls.description: Documentation and information about AI systems shall be provided to users and other interested parties at a level of detail proportionate to the AI system's impact — intended purpose, known limitations, how to interpret outputs.

controls.title: External reporting
controls.description: The organisation shall define what information about AI systems is reported externally, to whom, how frequently, and through what mechanism — regulatory disclosures, transparency reports, public-facing AI disclosures.

controls.title: Communication of incidents
controls.description: AI-specific incident communication processes — internal escalation, notification to affected individuals where required, regulatory reporting, post-incident review and disclosure.

controls.title: Information for interested parties
controls.description: Stakeholders — customers, employees, regulators, public — shall be informed of their rights and what to expect from AI systems. A communication plan addresses what to communicate, when, and through which channel.

controls.title: Processes for responsible use
controls.description: Formal processes shall govern how the organisation uses AI systems responsibly — acceptable use criteria, human oversight of AI outputs, procedures for escalating or overriding AI decisions.

controls.title: Objectives for responsible use
controls.description: Measurable objectives for responsible AI use shall be established and monitored, aligned to AI policy and responsible AI principles. These objectives drive continual improvement in AI use practices.

controls.title: Intended use of the AI system
controls.description: Intended use of each AI system shall be documented, communicated, and enforced. Use beyond the documented intended purpose shall be identified, assessed, and controlled.

controls.title: Allocating responsibilities
controls.description: Where AI systems involve multiple parties (providers, users, customers, partners), responsibilities for responsible AI governance shall be clearly allocated and documented — impact assessment, monitoring, incident response.

controls.title: Suppliers
controls.description: AI supply chain risk management shall govern third-party AI providers — tiering by risk, due diligence questionnaires, contractual AI-specific clauses (data handling, bias controls, right to audit), ongoing supplier performance monitoring.

controls.title: Customers
controls.description: Where AI systems are deployed to customers, define what information customers receive about AI capabilities and limitations, customer obligations in responsible use, and how customer feedback and incidents are managed.
